evals.query: Set up OpenTelemetry tracing in my Kora service and export traces to Jaeger over gRPC. The service name should be order-service.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:opentelemetry-tracing-exporter-grpc artifact and keeps it BOM-versioned via kora-parent (no explicit version on the artifact)
Extends OpentelemetryGrpcExporterModule from package ru.tinkoff.kora.opentelemetry.tracing.exporter.grpc on the @KoraApp interface
Sets tracing.exporter.endpoint to a Jaeger gRPC URL on port 4317 and tracing.attributes."service.name" to order-service
Includes the mandatory annotation processor (annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" for Java, ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" for Kotlin)
Does not use foreign-framework annotations, field injection, or a foreign application-bootstrap annotation

evals.query: I want to send traces to Zipkin at http://zipkin:9411 using the HTTP exporter instead of gRPC.
expected_behavior: Uses the ru.tinkoff.kora:opentelemetry-tracing-exporter-http artifact and extends OpentelemetryHttpExporterModule
Sets tracing.exporter.endpoint to http://zipkin:9411/api/v2/spans
Sets tracing.exporter.compression to "none" for Zipkin
Does not invent a tracing.exporter.protocol config key (protocol is determined by the chosen module)

evals.query: Add a manual span around my processOrder business method that records exceptions and reports trace status.
expected_behavior: Injects io.opentelemetry.api.trace.Tracer via the constructor of a @Component
Reads ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Context.current() and OpentelemetryContext.get(ctx), then parents the span with setParent(otctx.getContext())
Registers the span with OpentelemetryContext.set(ctx, otctx.add(span)) and restores the previous context in finally
Calls span.recordException(e) and span.setStatus(StatusCode.ERROR, ...) on failure and span.end() in finally
Does not use field injection or @Autowired

evals.query: How do I keep the trace context connected when I move work to a CompletableFuture.supplyAsync in a Kora service?
expected_behavior: Calls Context.current().fork() before the async hop to copy the Kora context
Calls OpentelemetryContext.set(...) inside the async body so the span becomes current on the worker thread
Ends the span in whenComplete (or finally) and sets OK/ERROR status
Does not rely on a foreign @Async-style mechanism or a ThreadLocal-only propagation assumption

evals.query: In my Kotlin Kora service I have a suspend function and I want the manual span to stay inside the request trace.
expected_behavior: Builds the span with setParent(otctx.context) from OpentelemetryContext.get(ctx)
Wraps the suspend body in withContext(Context.Kotlin.asCoroutineContext(ctx))
Restores the previous OpentelemetryContext and calls span.end() in finally
Uses constructor injection of Tracer in a @Component, not field injection

evals.query: I only want to sample about 10% of traces in production. How do I configure the sampler in Kora?
expected_behavior: Explains that sampling is not a HOCON config key and there is no tracing.sampler block
Overrides opentelemetryTracingSampler() in the @KoraApp interface to shadow the @DefaultComponent
Returns a Sampler from io.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.samplers, e.g. Sampler.parentBased(Sampler.traceIdRatioBased(0.1))
Does not fabricate tracing.sampler.probability or tracing.sampler.type keys

evals.query: Configure tracing export to Grafana Tempo over OTLP gRPC.
expected_behavior: Uses OpentelemetryGrpcExporterModule with tracing.exporter.endpoint set to a Tempo gRPC URL on port 4317
Sets tracing.exporter.compression to "none" for Tempo
Keeps tracing.attributes."service.name" set so the service is findable in the UI

evals.query: I keep repeating the span boilerplate everywhere. Give me a reusable component that wraps any operation in a span with correct context handling.
expected_behavior: Creates a @Component (e.g. TracingService) with a generic method taking an operation name and a Supplier/lambda
Manages the full span lifecycle: setParent on the request context, OpentelemetryContext.add/set, status, recordException, span.end() and context restore in finally
Injects Tracer through the constructor
Does not use foreign-framework AOP, @Aspect, or field injection
